Hi, James, I don't think we should separate a 3.12 series in ldiskfs directory. We should keep it as clear as possible. As you know, for rhel7 and sles12, They are all real distro, So we will update patches as distro needed. Eventually some patch maybe useless in 3.12 directory. So we may need a extra work to check & remove them. Also i don't think 3.12 is good choice as a base set. I am also wonder why it can help commit patch to upstream. This patch set i have update a few problem i have found in local test. Mainly in data-in-dirent patch. Please focus on it.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

I strongly disagree with you about the issue of sharing files between distros. In the past fixes for RHEL never ended up in SuSE but that changed when patches started to be shared. The only issue is when new patches are introduced. As for why using 3.12 as a base was due to SuSE12 being nearly identical to 3.12. Only RHEL has a tendency to drift over time from the baseline it uses. For the SuSE 3.0 series very little has changed so this shows that the 3.12 patches will not decay as you think. Same for Fedora, which also does little back porting but instead moves forward. Once the ldiskfs patch start migrating upstream they might change form which will most likely be feed back into our patches here.Using a standard base will be easier to work with in this case.

Patch Set 4:

James, The first rule is to keep minimize patch numbers for ldiskfs. As you say, SLES12 almost identical to 3.12. Why we need separate a 3.12 directory? It just cause extra effort. We should keep share patches among the distro rather than fork them. I don't think 3.12 so decay as a base set. But it just dones't need at all. Also i don't think keep a 3.12 set can help upstream things like commit to or back port from either.

(6 comments)

Many of these patches are still missing descriptions at the start of what they do. After years of asking for that, I would have thought they would all have descriptions by now. 

I think more could be done to remove some of these patches rather than just continue to port them to new kernels each time.

Some, like nocmtime could probably be accepted upstream. Others could at least be reduced in size with some cleanups to Lustre or the patch. 

Given the late stage for getting this into 2.7.0 it looks like we are again going to go forward with this full patch series, but it would be nice to at least mske some progress in reducing the number of patches in the future.
